steps.label: cause
steps.text: Young wine is packaged in a flexible inner bag fitted with a dispensing tap and protected by an outer box.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: As wine is dispensed the bag collapses, limiting replacement air, while lightweight packaging lowers transport and serving costs.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The format is practical for affordable wines consumed over a relatively short period after purchase and can preserve an opened wine better than an air-filled bottle.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: a collapsible bag limits air entry and offers economical, practical service for young everyday wine.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
